name: Fried 100-year Old Eggs
recipeInstructions:
1. Scrape ashes from one of the 100-year-old eggs (B) with a knife, wash thoroughly.
2. Cover B with water, bring to a boil and cook for 3 minutes. Drain, cover with cold water for a few seconds. Shell and quarter B.
3. Break the yolk of another 100-year-old egg (C) into a fork and beat until fluffy.
4. Gradually mix in flour from C, add sugar, wine vinegar, salt, and tomatoes. Mix well.
5. Dip the remaining 100-year-old eggs (H) in boiling water for a few seconds, remove skin, and cut into slices.
6. Heat peanut oil to 375°F.
7. Dip each B and H in the mixture C-G until well coated. Deep-fry until all sides are golden brown.
8. Place cooked B and H in the center of a dish. Garnish with tomato slices. Serve hot or cold.
